ddls:
CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "geoid" TEXT /* Geographic ID of the state (GEOID) */,
  "geoid2" INTEGER /* Alternate geographic ID of the state (GEOID2) */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* Two-letter code for each state */,
  "state_capital" TEXT /* Capital city of each state */,
  "state_area" TEXT /* Total area of each state in square miles */,
  "state_population_density" TEXT /* Population density of each state (people per square mile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_data_years" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state the data belongs to */,
  "data_year" INTEGER /* Year to which the population data belongs */,
  "population_estimate" INTEGER /* Population estimate for the specified year */,
  "population_change" INTEGER /* Change in population from the previous year */,
  "population_growth_rate" TEXT /* Growth rate of the population from the previous year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_years_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demo_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state the demographic data belongs to */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., 0-17, 18-64, 65+) */,
  "male_population" INTEGER /* Male population for the age group */,
  "female_population" INTEGER /* Female population for the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demo_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "race_and_ethnicity" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race/ethnicity entry */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state the race/ethnicity data belongs to */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race or ethnicity (e.g., White, Black or African American, Asian) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the specified race or ethnicity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_race_and_ethnicity_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education" (
  "edu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education entry */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state the education data belongs to */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level (e.g., High School, Bachelor's Degree) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population with the specified education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("edu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)
